---
title: "À Paris"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r packages, include=FALSE}
rm(list = ls())
pkgs.load <- c("dplyr", "leaflet", "stringr", "tidyr", "tidyverse", "RColorBrewer", "DT", "openxlsx2")
lapply(pkgs.load, require, character.only = TRUE)
```

```{r data, include=FALSE}
# 1. Load the workbook object
wb <- wb_load("Paris.xlsx")

# 2. Extract Data: One for text, one for raw hyperlinks
df_text  <- wb_to_df(wb, show_hyperlinks = FALSE)
df_links <- wb_to_df(wb, show_hyperlinks = TRUE)
##### DEBUG #####
# debug.df <- wb_to_df(wb,show_hyperlinks = TRUE)
# test.df <- Paris <- read_excel("~/Downloads/Paris.xlsx")

# 3. Clean headers and keep ONLY core columns to avoid "NA" or duplicate column errors
# Use lowercase to match the rest of your script
colnames(df_text)  <- tolower(make.names(colnames(df_text), unique = TRUE))
colnames(df_links) <- tolower(make.names(colnames(df_links), unique = TRUE))

# Select only what is needed (adjust names if your Excel headers differ)
Paris <- df_text %>% select(name, note, category, coordinates)
urls  <- df_links$name  # Targets from the 'name' column

# 4. Map Hyperlinks to the Name Column
Paris$name <- mapply(function(txt, link) {
  if (!is.na(link) && link != txt) {
    # target='_blank' ensures links open in a new tab
    return(paste0("<a href='", link, "' target='_blank'>", txt, "</a>"))
  } else {
    return(txt)
  }
}, Paris$name, urls)
```

```{r processing, include=FALSE}
# Coordinate Extraction and Cleaning
Paris <- Paris %>%
  mutate(
    lng_val = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", str_extract(coordinates, ",.*"))),
    lat_val = as.numeric(gsub(",", "", str_extract(coordinates, "^.*,")))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Create the caption using the new linked name
    caption = paste(replace_na(name, ""), replace_na(note, ""), sep = " | "),
    caption = str_replace_all(caption, "NA", ""),
    caption = str_trim(str_replace(caption, "^\\| | \\|$", ""))
  ) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(lat_val), !is.na(lng_val))

categories <- sort(unique(Paris$category))

# Row Removal example
Paris <- Paris %>% filter(name != "Chartres Cathedral")
```

```{r leaflet_map, echo=FALSE, out.width = "100%", out.height = "600px"}
# Expanded Palette
color_count <- length(categories)
get_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(9, "Set1"))
custom_pal  <- colorFactor(palette = get_palette(color_count), domain = categories)

# Build Map
m <- leaflet(data = Paris) %>%
  addProviderTiles(providers$OpenStreetMap) %>%
  addLayersControl(
    overlayGroups = categories,
    options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = TRUE),
    position = "topright"
  ) %>%
  addLegend(
    pal = custom_pal, values = categories, 
    title = "Category", position = "topleft"
  )

# Add Markers via Loop
for(cat in categories) {
  df_sub <- Paris %>% filter(category == cat)
  m <- m %>% addCircleMarkers(
    data = df_sub, lat = ~lat_val, lng = ~lng_val,
    color = ~custom_pal(category), opacity = 1, fillOpacity = 0.8,
    radius = 8, popup = ~caption, group = cat
  )
}
m
```

```{r table, echo=FALSE}
# Display the searchable table
datatable(
  Paris %>% select(name, note, category),
  escape = FALSE, # MANDATORY to render the <a> links
  options = list(paging = FALSE, scrollY = "400px", dom = "ft"),
  rownames = FALSE,
  colnames = c("Location", "Note", "Category")
)

write_csv(x = Paris, file = "Paris_out.csv")
openxlsx2::write_xlsx(x = Paris,file = "Paris_out.xlsx")
```
